campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to support youth scholarships in the recreation program. The council also discussed plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue and considered extending a temporary local ordinance.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
City applying for grant funding to study the Hamilton Avenue bridge project
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 under consideration

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a new rule or policy the city is considering. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (city rule) and decided to fast-track it for approval at their next meeting instead of going through the normal slower review process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading requirement to speed up approval timeline
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next regular council meeting
New city rule being pushed to consent calendar for expedited passage

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from multiple sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. This was largely a housekeeping meeting focused on official record-keeping and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved official records from six March meetings
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Mix of public and closed session meetings reviewed

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve several infrastructure and service agreements for the city. The main items included updates to the airport's baggage handling system contract, plans for managing affordable housing if subsidy programs end, improvements to the storm sewer system, and progress on a FEMA-funded family emergency shelter project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system upgrade with Vanderlande Industries
Protecting affordable housing if federal/project subsidies expire
Storm sewer system master planning with Wood Rodgers
FEMA Family Camp emergency shelter project

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a study session to review Mountain View's spending plans for building and improving public infrastructure over the next fiscal year. They also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Planning and approval of capital improvement spending

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These recognitions highlight the city's commitment to celebrating diverse communities and ensuring equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Equal housing opportunity initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows the public how Cupertino spends its money. The council also reminded people about rules requiring lobbyists to register with the city if they try to influence local government decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration
City budget information now available to public
Lobbyist registration requirements under city law
How to report lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included an important legal notice for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue, you have 90 days from when the decision is finalized to file a lawsuit—if you wait longer, you'll lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for council meetings
How to challenge city quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to handle several administrative matters including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and a review of new project requests from individual council members. The council also discussed changes to how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 2025-2030 federal funding and consolidated plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy ordinance
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking the city to officially recognize the building as more historically significant and wants permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space as an incentive for fixing up the building and making it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building getting new historic designation
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic improvements
Downtown Commercial zone downtown development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 12
